maml:description.maml:para: Adds the specified configuration to the current logging configuration.
The Add-NLogLoggingConfiguration cmdlet adds the specified configuration instance to the static `NLog.LogManager.Configuration` property.
The cmdlet will throw if an existing configuration is already set. Use the Stop-NLogLogging cmdlet to shut down logging before setting a new configuration.
Specifies the configuration to apply.
Returns the configuration object. By default, this cmdlet does not generate any output.
Specifies the configuration to apply.
Returns the configuration object. By default, this cmdlet does not generate any output.
You can pipe an **NLog.Config.LoggingConfiguration** object to this cmdlet.
When you use the **PassThru** parameter, this cmdlet returns the **NLog.Config.LoggingConfiguration** object. Otherwise, this cmdlet does not generate any output.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 1: Add a configuration to the static `NLog.LogManager.Configuration` property ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell $configuration = New-NLogLoggingConfiguration $target = New-NLogColoredConsoleTarget -Name ConsoleTarget $rule = New-NLogLoggingRule ` -Target $target ` -Name ConsoleRule ` -LoggerNamePattern * ` -MinLevel Trace ` -MaxLevel Fatal Add-NLogLoggingRule -Configuration $configuration -Rule $rule Add-NLogLoggingConfiguration $configuration ```
This example creates a basic configuration with a console target and adds it to the static `NLog.LogManager.Configuration` property.

maml:description.maml:para: Adds the specified logging rule to the specified configuration.
The Add-NLogLoggingRule cmdlet adds the specified logging rule to the existing collection of rules in the specified configuration.
Specifies the configuration to update.
Returns the configuration object. By default, this cmdlet does not generate any output.
Specifies the rule to add.
Specifies the configuration to update.
Returns the configuration object. By default, this cmdlet does not generate any output.
Specifies the rule to add.
You can pipe an **NLog.Config.LoggingRule** object to this cmdlet.
You can pipe an **NLog.Config.LoggingConfiguration** object to this cmdlet.
When you use the **PassThru** parameter, this cmdlet returns the **NLog.Config.LoggingConfiguration** object. Otherwise, this cmdlet does not generate any output.

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell $configuration = New-NLogLoggingConfiguration $target = New-NLogColoredConsoleTarget -Name ConsoleTarget $rule = New-NLogLoggingRule ` -Target $target ` -Name ConsoleRule ` -LoggerNamePattern * ` -MinLevel Trace ` -MaxLevel Fatal Add-NLogLoggingRule -Configuration $configuration -Rule $rule Add-NLogLoggingConfiguration $configuration ```
This example creates a rule for a console target.

maml:description.maml:para: Adds the specified logging target to the specified configuration.
The Add-NLogTarget cmdlet adds the specified logging target to the existing collection of targets in the specified configuration.
Specifies the configuration to update.
Returns the configuration object. By default, this cmdlet does not generate any output.
Specifies the target to add.
Specifies the configuration to update.
Returns the configuration object. By default, this cmdlet does not generate any output.
Specifies the target to add.
You can pipe an **NLog.Targets.Target** object to this cmdlet.
You can pipe an **NLog.Config.LoggingConfiguration** object to this cmdlet.
When you use the **PassThru** parameter, this cmdlet returns the **NLog.Config.LoggingConfiguration** object. Otherwise, this cmdlet does not generate any output.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 1: Add a target to a configuration ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell $configuration = New-NLogLoggingConfiguration $consoleTarget = New-NLogColoredConsoleTarget -Name ConsoleTarget Add-NLogTarget -Target $consoleTarget -Configuration $configuration ```
This example adds a console target to the configuration.

maml:description.maml:para: Gets a named logger.
The Get-NLogLogger cmdlet gets a named logger from the current configuration.
Specifies the name of the logger.
Specifies the name of the logger.
You can pipe the logger name to this cmdlet.
This cmdlet returns an **NLog.Logger** object.

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell $configuration = New-NLogLoggingConfiguration $target = New-NLogColoredConsoleTarget -Name ConsoleTarget $rule = New-NLogLoggingRule ` -Target $target ` -Name ConsoleRule ` -LoggerNamePattern * ` -MinLevel Trace ` -MaxLevel Fatal Add-NLogLoggingRule -Configuration $configuration -Rule $rule Add-NLogLoggingConfiguration $configuration $logger = Get-NLogLogger -Name MyLogger ```
This example creates a console logger named MyLogger.

maml:description.maml:para: Gets the current logging configuration.
The Get-NLogLoggingConfiguration cmdlet gets the current configuration.
The cmdlet will throw if a configuration is not found.
This cmdlet returns the **NLog.Config.LoggingConfiguration** object from the static `NLog.LogManager.Configuration` property.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 1: Gets the previously set configuration from the static `NLog.LogManager.Configuration` property ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell $configuration = Get-NLogLoggingConfiguration ```
This example gets the previously set configuration from the static `NLog.LogManager.Configuration` property.

maml:description.maml:para: Creates a basic logger with a common configuration.
The New-NLogBasicLogger cmdlet creates a basic logger with a common configuration.
This cmdlet performs the following functions:

- Creates a new NLog configuration with a console and file target. - Assigns the new configuration to the static NLog logging configuration. - Creates a new NLog logger. - Returns the logger instance.
The name of the logger.
The name of the log file.
The name of the logger.
The name of the log file.
You can pipe a string to this cmdlet.
This cmdlet returns an **NLog.Config.LoggingConfiguration** object.

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell $logger = New-NLogBasicLogger -Name MyLogger -Path x:/path/application.log ```
This command creates a new logger with the MyLogger name and writes log entries to the console and indicated path.

maml:description.maml:para: Creates a colored console target.
The New-NLogColoredConsoleTarget cmdlet creates a colored console target.
Gets or sets a value indicating whether to auto-flush after M:System.Console.WriteLine.

Gets or sets a value indicating whether to auto-check if the console is available. - Disables console writing if Environment.UserInteractive = false (Windows Service) - Disables console writing if Console Standard Input is not available (Non-Console-App).
Gets or sets a value indicating whether to auto-check if the console has been redirected to file - Disables coloring logic when System.Console.IsOutputRedirected = true.
Enables output using ANSI Color Codes.
The encoding for writing messages to the T:System.Console.
Gets or sets the footer.
Gets or sets the header.
Gets or sets the layout used to format log messages.
Gets or sets the name of the target.
Support NO_COLOR=1 environment variable. See also https://no-color.org/.
Gets the row highlighting rules.
Gets or sets a value indicating whether to send the log messages to the standard error instead of the standard output.
Gets or sets a value indicating whether to use default row highlighting rules.
Gets the word highlighting rules.
Gets or sets a value indicating whether to auto-flush after M:System.Console.WriteLine.

Gets or sets a value indicating whether to auto-check if the console is available. - Disables console writing if Environment.UserInteractive = false (Windows Service) - Disables console writing if Console Standard Input is not available (Non-Console-App).
Gets or sets a value indicating whether to auto-check if the console has been redirected to file - Disables coloring logic when System.Console.IsOutputRedirected = true.
Enables output using ANSI Color Codes.
The encoding for writing messages to the T:System.Console.
Gets or sets the footer.
Gets or sets the header.
Gets or sets the layout used to format log messages.
Gets or sets the name of the target.
Support NO_COLOR=1 environment variable. See also https://no-color.org/.
Gets the row highlighting rules.
Gets or sets a value indicating whether to send the log messages to the standard error instead of the standard output.
Gets or sets a value indicating whether to use default row highlighting rules.
Gets the word highlighting rules.
You can pipe a Boolean value to this cmdlet.
You can pipe a **System.Text.Encoding** object to this cmdlet.
You can pipe an **NLog.Layouts.Layout** object to this cmdlet.
You can pipe a string to this cmdlet.
You can pipe a typed **NLog.Layouts.Layout** object to this cmdlet.
You can pipe an **NLog.Targets.ConsoleRowHighlightingRule** object to this cmdlet.
You can pipe an **NLog.Targets.ConsoleWordHighlightingRule** object to this cmdlet.
You can pipe a typed **NLog.Layouts.Layout** object to this cmdlet.
You can pipe an array of **NLog.Targets.ConsoleRowHighlightingRule** objects to this cmdlet.
You can pipe an array of **NLog.Targets.ConsoleWordHighlightingRule** objects to this cmdlet.
This cmdlet returns an **NLog.Targets.ColoredConsoleTarget** object.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 1: Create a colored console target ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell $configuration = New-NLogLoggingConfiguration $target = New-NLogColoredConsoleTarget -Name ConsoleTarget $rule = New-NLogLoggingRule ` -Target $target ` -Name ConsoleRule ` -LoggerNamePattern * ` -MinLevel Trace ` -MaxLevel Fatal Add-NLogLoggingRule -Configuration $configuration -Rule $rule Add-NLogLoggingConfiguration $configuration $logger = Get-NLogLogger -Name MyLogger ```
This example creates a logger with a colored console target.

maml:description.maml:para: Creates a colored console row highlighting rule.
The New-NLogConsoleRowHighlightingRule cmdlet creates a colored console row highlighting rule.
Gets or sets the background color.
Gets or sets the condition that must be met in order to set the specified foreground and background color.
Gets or sets the foreground color.
Gets or sets the background color.
Gets or sets the condition that must be met in order to set the specified foreground and background color.
Gets or sets the foreground color.
You can pipe a **ConsoleOutputColor** value to this cmdlet.
You can pipe an **NLog.Conditions.ConditionExpression** object to this cmdlet.
This cmdlet returns an **NLog.Targets.ConsoleRowHighlightingRule** object.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 1: Create a row highlighting rule for Fatal entries ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell $rule = New-NLogConsoleRowHighlightingRule ` -Condition 'level == LogLevel.Fatal' ` -ForegroundColor White ` -BackgroundColor Red $target = New-NLogColoredConsoleTarget -Name ConsoleTarget -RowHighlightingRules $rule ```
This example creates a row highlighting rule that displays Fatal log entries with white text on a red background, then applies it to a colored console target.

maml:description.maml:para: Creates a console target.
The New-NLogConsoleTarget cmdlet creates a console target.
Gets or sets a value indicating whether to auto-flush after M:System.Console.WriteLine.

Gets or sets a value indicating whether to auto-check if the console is available - Disables console writing if Environment.UserInteractive = false (Windows Service) - Disables console writing if Console Standard Input is not available (Non-Console-App).
The encoding for writing messages to the T:System.Console.
Gets or sets the footer.
Gets or sets whether to force M:System.Console.WriteLine (slower) instead of the faster internal buffering.
Gets or sets the header.
Gets or sets the layout used to format log messages.
Gets or sets the name of the target.
Gets or sets a value indicating whether to send the log messages to the standard error instead of the standard output.
Gets or sets a value indicating whether to auto-flush after M:System.Console.WriteLine.

Gets or sets a value indicating whether to auto-check if the console is available - Disables console writing if Environment.UserInteractive = false (Windows Service) - Disables console writing if Console Standard Input is not available (Non-Console-App).
The encoding for writing messages to the T:System.Console.
Gets or sets the footer.
Gets or sets whether to force M:System.Console.WriteLine (slower) instead of the faster internal buffering.
Gets or sets the header.
Gets or sets the layout used to format log messages.
Gets or sets the name of the target.
Gets or sets a value indicating whether to send the log messages to the standard error instead of the standard output.
You can pipe a Boolean value to this cmdlet.
You can pipe a **System.Text.Encoding** object to this cmdlet.
You can pipe an **NLog.Layouts.Layout** object to this cmdlet.
You can pipe a string to this cmdlet.
You can pipe a typed **NLog.Layouts.Layout** object to this cmdlet.
You can pipe a typed **NLog.Layouts.Layout** object to this cmdlet.
This cmdlet returns an **NLog.Targets.ConsoleTarget** object.

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell $configuration = New-NLogLoggingConfiguration $target = New-NLogConsoleTarget -Name ConsoleTarget $rule = New-NLogLoggingRule ` -Target $target ` -Name ConsoleRule ` -LoggerNamePattern * ` -MinLevel Trace ` -MaxLevel Fatal Add-NLogLoggingRule -Configuration $configuration -Rule $rule Add-NLogLoggingConfiguration $configuration $logger = Get-NLogLogger -Name MyLogger ```
This example creates a logger with a console target.

maml:description.maml:para: Creates a colored console word highlighting rule.
The New-NLogConsoleWordHighlightingRule cmdlet creates a colored console word highlighting rule.
Gets or sets the background color.
Gets or sets the condition that must be met before scanning the row for highlight of words.
Gets or sets the foreground color.
Gets or sets a value indicating whether to ignore case when comparing texts.
Gets or sets the text to be matched for Highlighting.
Gets or sets a value indicating whether to match whole words only.
Gets or sets the list of words to be matched for Highlighting.
Gets or sets the background color.
Gets or sets the condition that must be met before scanning the row for highlight of words.
Gets or sets the foreground color.
Gets or sets a value indicating whether to ignore case when comparing texts.
Gets or sets the text to be matched for Highlighting.
Gets or sets a value indicating whether to match whole words only.
Gets or sets the list of words to be matched for Highlighting.
You can pipe a **ConsoleOutputColor** value to this cmdlet.
You can pipe an **NLog.Conditions.ConditionExpression** object to this cmdlet.
You can pipe a Boolean value to this cmdlet.
You can pipe a string to this cmdlet.
You can pipe an array of strings to this cmdlet.
This cmdlet returns an **NLog.Targets.ConsoleWordHighlightingRule** object.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 1: Create a word highlighting rule ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell $rule = New-NLogConsoleWordHighlightingRule ` -Text 'error' ` -IgnoreCase $true ` -ForegroundColor Red $target = New-NLogColoredConsoleTarget -Name ConsoleTarget -WordHighlightingRules $rule ```
This example creates a word highlighting rule that displays the word "error" in red regardless of case, then applies it to a colored console target.

maml:description.maml:para: Creates a Windows Event Log target.
The New-NLogEventLogTarget cmdlet creates a Windows Event Log target.
Gets or sets the layout that renders event Category.
Optional entry type. When not set, or when not convertible to T:System.Diagnostics.EventLogEntryType then determined by T:NLog.LogLevel.
Gets or sets the layout that renders event ID.
Gets or sets the layout used to format log messages.
Gets or sets the name of the Event Log to write to. This can be System, Application or any user-defined name.
Gets or sets the name of the machine on which Event Log service is running.
Gets or sets the maximum Event log size in kilobytes.
Gets or sets the message length limit to write to the Event Log.
Gets or sets the name of the target.
Gets or sets the action to take if the message is larger than the P:NLog.Targets.EventLogTarget.MaxMessageLength option.
Gets or sets the value to be used as the event Source.
Gets or sets the layout that renders event Category.
Optional entry type. When not set, or when not convertible to T:System.Diagnostics.EventLogEntryType then determined by T:NLog.LogLevel.
Gets or sets the layout that renders event ID.
Gets or sets the layout used to format log messages.
Gets or sets the name of the Event Log to write to. This can be System, Application or any user-defined name.
Gets or sets the name of the machine on which Event Log service is running.
Gets or sets the maximum Event log size in kilobytes.
Gets or sets the message length limit to write to the Event Log.
Gets or sets the name of the target.
Gets or sets the action to take if the message is larger than the P:NLog.Targets.EventLogTarget.MaxMessageLength option.
Gets or sets the value to be used as the event Source.
You can pipe a typed **NLog.Layouts.Layout** object to this cmdlet.
You can pipe a typed **NLog.Layouts.Layout** object to this cmdlet.
You can pipe a typed **NLog.Layouts.Layout** object to this cmdlet.
You can pipe an **NLog.Layouts.Layout** object to this cmdlet.
You can pipe a typed **NLog.Layouts.Layout** object to this cmdlet.
You can pipe a string to this cmdlet.
You can pipe an **EventLogTargetOverflowAction** value to this cmdlet.
You can pipe a typed **NLog.Layouts.Layout** object to this cmdlet.
You can pipe a typed **NLog.Layouts.Layout** object to this cmdlet.
You can pipe a typed **NLog.Layouts.Layout** object to this cmdlet.
You can pipe a typed **NLog.Layouts.Layout** object to this cmdlet.
This cmdlet returns an **NLog.Targets.EventLogTarget** object.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 1: Create an Event Log target ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell $configuration = New-NLogLoggingConfiguration $target = New-NLogEventLogTarget -Source MySource -Name EventLogTarget $rule = New-NLogLoggingRule ` -Target $target ` -Name EventLogRule ` -LoggerNamePattern * ` -MinLevel Trace ` -MaxLevel Fatal Add-NLogLoggingRule -Configuration $configuration -Rule $rule Add-NLogLoggingConfiguration $configuration $logger.WithProperty('EventID', 42).Info('Hello World!') ```
This example creates a logger with an Event Log target.

maml:description.maml:para: Creates a file target.
The New-NLogFileTarget cmdlet creates a file target.
Gets or sets the size in bytes above which log files will be automatically archived. Zero or negative means disabled.
Gets or sets a value indicating whether to trigger archive operation based on time-period, by moving active-file to file-path specified by P:NLog.Targets.FileTarget.ArchiveFileName.

Legacy archive logic where file-archive-logic moves active file to path specified by P:NLog.Targets.FileTarget.ArchiveFileName, and then recreates the active file. Use P:NLog.Targets.FileTarget.ArchiveSuffixFormat to control suffix format, instead of now obsolete token {#}.
Gets or sets a value indicating whether any existing log-file should be archived on startup.
Gets or sets the format-string to convert archive sequence-number by using string.Format.
Gets or sets a value indicating whether to automatically flush the file buffers after each log message.
Gets or sets the log file buffer size in bytes.
Gets or sets a value indicating whether to create directories if they do not exist.
Gets or sets a value indicating whether to delete old log file on startup.
Gets or sets whether or not this target should just discard all data that its asked to write. Mostly used for when testing NLog Stack except final write.
Gets or sets a value indicating whether to enable log file(s) to be deleted.
Gets or sets the file encoding.
Gets or sets the name of the file to write to.
Gets or sets the footer.
Gets or sets the header.
Gets or sets a value indicating whether to keep log file open instead of opening and closing it on each logging event.
Gets or sets the layout used to format log messages.
Gets or sets the line ending mode.
Gets or sets the maximum days of archive files that should be kept. Zero or negative means disabled.
Gets or sets the maximum number of archive files that should be kept. Negative means disabled.
Gets or sets the name of the target.
Gets or sets the maximum number of files to be kept open.
Gets or sets the maximum number of seconds that files are kept open. Zero or negative means disabled.
Gets or sets the maximum number of seconds before open files are flushed. Zero or negative means disabled.
Gets or sets a value indicating whether to replace file contents on each write instead of appending log message at the end.
Gets or sets a value indicating whether to write BOM (byte order mark) in created files.
Gets or sets a value indicating whether the footer should be written only when the file is archived.

Gets or sets whether to write the Header on initial creation of file appender, even if the file is not empty. Default value is false, which means only write header when initial file is empty (Ex. ensures valid CSV files).
Gets or sets the size in bytes above which log files will be automatically archived. Zero or negative means disabled.
Gets or sets a value indicating whether to trigger archive operation based on time-period, by moving active-file to file-path specified by P:NLog.Targets.FileTarget.ArchiveFileName.

Legacy archive logic where file-archive-logic moves active file to path specified by P:NLog.Targets.FileTarget.ArchiveFileName, and then recreates the active file. Use P:NLog.Targets.FileTarget.ArchiveSuffixFormat to control suffix format, instead of now obsolete token {#}.
Gets or sets a value indicating whether any existing log-file should be archived on startup.
Gets or sets the format-string to convert archive sequence-number by using string.Format.
Gets or sets a value indicating whether to automatically flush the file buffers after each log message.
Gets or sets the log file buffer size in bytes.
Gets or sets a value indicating whether to create directories if they do not exist.
Gets or sets a value indicating whether to delete old log file on startup.
Gets or sets whether or not this target should just discard all data that its asked to write. Mostly used for when testing NLog Stack except final write.
Gets or sets a value indicating whether to enable log file(s) to be deleted.
Gets or sets the file encoding.
Gets or sets the name of the file to write to.
Gets or sets the footer.
Gets or sets the header.
Gets or sets a value indicating whether to keep log file open instead of opening and closing it on each logging event.
Gets or sets the layout used to format log messages.
Gets or sets the line ending mode.
Gets or sets the maximum days of archive files that should be kept. Zero or negative means disabled.
Gets or sets the maximum number of archive files that should be kept. Negative means disabled.
Gets or sets the name of the target.
Gets or sets the maximum number of files to be kept open.
Gets or sets the maximum number of seconds that files are kept open. Zero or negative means disabled.
Gets or sets the maximum number of seconds before open files are flushed. Zero or negative means disabled.
Gets or sets a value indicating whether to replace file contents on each write instead of appending log message at the end.
Gets or sets a value indicating whether to write BOM (byte order mark) in created files.
Gets or sets a value indicating whether the footer should be written only when the file is archived.

Gets or sets whether to write the Header on initial creation of file appender, even if the file is not empty. Default value is false, which means only write header when initial file is empty (Ex. ensures valid CSV files).
You can pipe an **Int64** value to this cmdlet.
You can pipe a **FileArchivePeriod** value to this cmdlet.
You can pipe an **NLog.Layouts.Layout** object to this cmdlet.
You can pipe a Boolean value to this cmdlet.
You can pipe a string to this cmdlet.
You can pipe an **Int32** value to this cmdlet.
You can pipe a **System.Text.Encoding** object to this cmdlet.
You can pipe a **LineEndingMode** value to this cmdlet.
This cmdlet returns an **NLog.Targets.FileTarget** object.

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell $configuration = New-NLogLoggingConfiguration $target = New-NLogFileTarget -FileName 'x:\path\logfile.log' -Name FileTarget $rule = New-NLogLoggingRule ` -Target $target ` -Name FileRule ` -LoggerNamePattern * ` -MinLevel Trace ` -MaxLevel Fatal Add-NLogLoggingRule -Configuration $configuration -Rule $rule Add-NLogLoggingConfiguration $configuration $logger = Get-NLogLogger -Name MyLogger ```
This example creates a logger with a file target.

maml:description.maml:para: Creates a logging configuration.
The New-NLogLoggingConfiguration cmdlet creates a logging configuration that is either empty or configured by an XML logging configuration.
Specifies the path to an XML configuration file.
Specifies the path to an XML configuration file.
You can pipe the path to an XML configuration file to this cmdlet.
This cmdlet returns an **NLog.Config.LoggingConfiguration** object.

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell $configuration = New-NLogLoggingConfiguration ```
This example creates an empty configuration.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 2: Create an XML based configuration ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell $configuration = New-NLogLoggingConfiguration -Path 'x:\path\NLog.config' ```
This example creates an XML based configuration.

maml:description.maml:para: Creates a logging rule.
The New-NLogLoggingRule cmdlet creates a logging rule.
Specifies whether to quit processing further rules when this one matches.
Specifies the logger name pattern.
Specifies the name of the rule.
Specifies the target to be written to when the rule matches.
Specifies whether to quit processing further rules when this one matches.
Specifies the logger name pattern.
Specifies the minimum log level needed to trigger this rule.
Specifies the name of the rule.
Specifies the target to be written to when the rule matches.
Specifies whether to quit processing further rules when this one matches.
Specifies the logger name pattern.
Specifies the maximum log level needed to trigger this rule.
Specifies the minimum log level needed to trigger this rule.
Specifies the name of the rule.
Specifies the target to be written to when the rule matches.
Specifies whether to quit processing further rules when this one matches.
Specifies the logger name pattern.
Specifies the maximum log level needed to trigger this rule.
Specifies the minimum log level needed to trigger this rule.
Specifies the name of the rule.
Specifies the target to be written to when the rule matches.
You can pipe an **NLog.Targets.Target** object to this cmdlet.
You can pipe a string to this cmdlet.
You can pipe an **NLog.LogLevel** object to this cmdlet.
You can pipe a **SwitchParameter** value to this cmdlet.
This cmdlet returns an **NLog.Config.LoggingRule** object.

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell $configuration = New-NLogLoggingConfiguration $target = New-NLogColoredConsoleTarget -Name ConsoleTarget $rule = New-NLogLoggingRule ` -Target $target ` -Name ConsoleRule ` -LoggerNamePattern * ` -MinLevel Trace ` -MaxLevel Fatal Add-NLogLoggingRule -Configuration $configuration -Rule $rule ```
This example creates a rule for a console target and adds it to the specified configuration.

maml:description.maml:para: Removes a logging rule.
The Remove-NLogLoggingRule cmdlet removes a logging rule from the specified configuration either by name or reference.
Specifies the configuration to modify.
Specifies the rule to remove.
Specifies the configuration to modify.
Specifies the name of the rule to remove.
Specifies the configuration to modify.
Specifies the name of the rule to remove.
Specifies the rule to remove.
You can pipe an **NLog.Config.LoggingRule** object to this cmdlet.
You can pipe the rule name to this cmdlet.
You can pipe an **NLog.Config.LoggingConfiguration** object to this cmdlet.
This cmdlet does not generate any output.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 1: Remove a rule by reference ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell $configuration = New-NLogLoggingConfiguration $target = New-NLogColoredConsoleTarget -Name ConsoleTarget $rule = New-NLogLoggingRule ` -Target $target ` -Name ConsoleRule ` -LoggerNamePattern * ` -MinLevel Trace ` -MaxLevel Fatal Add-NLogLoggingRule -Configuration $configuration -Rule $rule Remove-NLogLoggingRule -Configuration $configuration -Rule $rule ```
This example creates a basic configuration with a console target, then removes the rule by reference.

maml:description.maml:para: Removes a target.
The Remove-NLogTarget cmdlet removes a target from the specified configuration either by name or reference.
Specifies the configuration to modify.
Returns the configuration object. By default, this cmdlet does not generate any output.
Specifies the target to remove.
Specifies the configuration to modify.
Specifies the name of the target to remove.
Returns the configuration object. By default, this cmdlet does not generate any output.
Specifies the configuration to modify.
Specifies the name of the target to remove.
Returns the configuration object. By default, this cmdlet does not generate any output.
Specifies the target to remove.
You can pipe an **NLog.Targets.Target** object to this cmdlet.
You can pipe the target name to this cmdlet.
You can pipe an **NLog.Config.LoggingConfiguration** object to this cmdlet.
When you use the **PassThru** parameter, this cmdlet returns the **NLog.Config.LoggingConfiguration** object. Otherwise, this cmdlet does not generate any output.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 1: Remove a target by reference ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell $configuration = New-NLogLoggingConfiguration $target = New-NLogColoredConsoleTarget -Name ConsoleTarget $rule = New-NLogLoggingRule ` -Target $target ` -Name ConsoleRule ` -LoggerNamePattern * ` -MinLevel Trace ` -MaxLevel Fatal Add-NLogLoggingRule -Configuration $configuration -Rule $rule Remove-NLogTarget -Configuration $configuration -Target $target ```
This example creates a basic configuration with a console target, then removes the target by reference.

maml:description.maml:para: Stops logging.
The Stop-NLogLogging cmdlet stops logging and clears the current logging configuration.
This cmdlet does not generate any output.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 1: Dispose all targets, and shuts logging down ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell Stop-NLogLogging ```
This example disposes all targets and shuts logging down. It assumes the configuration instance was set to the static `NLog.LogManager.Configuration` property.
